title: Psalms — Lesson 1: Introduction to the Power of Prayer

sections.opening:
Sit down, my dear, let's light a candle and enter together into the world of Psalms.

The truth is that each of us has recited Psalms at some point in our lives — perhaps in synagogue, perhaps beside a sick bed, perhaps in personal prayer on a difficult day. But what most people don't know is that every chapter in Psalms is like a specific spiritual key — for every situation there's its chapter, for every problem there's its solution.

Today we'll start from the basics: How do the Psalms work? Why did King David write exactly 150 chapters? And how do you know which chapter to say when? Maktuba will show you that Psalms isn't just beautiful text — it's precise spiritual science.

The people who walked before me understood that every word here carries a special spiritual frequency, that every chapter creates vibrations in higher worlds. Come, let's learn how to use this power.
sections.theoryA:
How do you choose which Psalm to say?

The first way is according to your age. The tradition of the Baal Shem Tov says that every person recites the chapter corresponding to their age plus one. If you're 25, you say chapter 26. If you're 30, you say chapter 31.

Why exactly this way? Because every year in your life brings with it different spiritual burdens, and every chapter of Psalms fixes exactly what your soul needs at that stage. This isn't coincidence — it's precise spiritual planning.

The second way is according to the Hebrew month. Every month has its zodiac sign, and every sign has a Psalm chapter that matches its energies. Tishrei (Libra) — chapter 81, Cheshvan (Scorpio) — chapter 80, Kislev (Sagittarius) — chapter 30.

But there's another way, more advanced — according to your need. Chapter 23 for livelihood, chapter 3 against fears, chapter 6 for healing the eyes. Every chapter is like a spiritual pharmacy — you need to know which remedy to take for which symptom.

The sages teach us that every chapter of Psalms was written in divine inspiration, meaning King David didn't just compose beautiful poems — he wove spiritual energies into word combinations that create precise effects in higher worlds.

practice1.instruction: Now I want you to think about your age and write which chapter of Psalms you need to say according to the Baal Shem Tov tradition (age + 1). If you're 28, write chapter 29. Remember this number.

sections.theoryB:
What's the connection between Psalms and astrology and Kabbalah?

The Psalms don't work in a vacuum. They're part of an enormous spiritual system that connects astrology, Kabbalah and numerology. Every Hebrew month begins with Rosh Chodesh (new moon), and at that time the astrological energies of that zodiac sign awaken.

For example, the month of Sivan is the sign of Gemini, and the chapter that corresponds to it is 119. Why? Because Gemini symbolizes communication and wisdom, and chapter 119 is the longest in Psalms — 176 verses divided according to the 22 Hebrew letters. It's the perfect spiritual nourishment for a sign that loves information and learning.

Kabbalah teaches that every Hebrew letter carries special spiritual power. Chapter 119 is built so that every 8 verses begin with the same letter — 8 verses with Aleph, 8 with Beth, and so on. The Chida wrote that it's a "wonderful segulah (spiritual remedy) beyond measure" to read verses according to the letters of your name.

Numerology also connects here. The number 150 (like the number of Psalm chapters) in Hebrew numerology symbolizes spiritual perfection. It divides into 6 (the number of days in which the world was created) times 25 (the number of years it takes for a soul to truly mature).

Every time you say Psalms with the right intention, you create a spiritual vibration that connects to the cosmic energies operating at that time. This isn't just prayer — it's scientific spiritual work.

practice2.instruction: Check which Hebrew month we're in now, and find which chapter of Psalms corresponds to this month. Say it with the intention that the energies of the zodiac sign work for your benefit. Pay attention to the feelings that arise during the reading.

sections.deepDive:
The secret not written in any book

What they won't tell you in study halls is that Psalms also work like a spiritual horoscope. Every chapter corresponds not only to specific problems, but also to certain character traits and precise times in life.

Another important thing to know: there's an enormous difference between saying Psalms as an "obligation" versus saying them as a "profession." When you understand exactly which chapter fits which situation, and how to direct the intention — the effect becomes much more precise and powerful.

There's also a secret of timing: there are chapters that work better in the morning (like chapter 5 — "morning prayer"), and there are those that work better at night (like chapter 3 — against fears). This depends on the heavenly energies that are active at each hour.

And the deepest secret? You can combine several chapters together and create a "spiritual recipe" customized personally. For example, chapter 23 for livelihood + chapter 29 for peace at home + the chapter according to your age = a perfect spiritual cocktail for your current situation.

Maktuba has been doing this for years, and I can tell you that when you start working with Psalms in this scientific way — life changes. Not immediately, not by magic, but in a way that you feel you have a real spiritual tool in your hands.

questions.question: According to the Baal Shem Tov tradition, a 35-year-old person should say which chapter of Psalms?
questions.explanation: The tradition says age + 1, meaning a 35-year-old says chapter 36.

questions.question: Which chapter of Psalms corresponds to the month of Kislev?
questions.explanation: Kislev is the sign of Sagittarius, and the corresponding chapter is 30.

questions.question: Chapter 119 is special because it:
questions.explanation: Chapter 119 is the longest with 176 verses, divided according to the 22 Hebrew letters.

questions.question: Which chapter of Psalms corresponds to livelihood?
questions.explanation: Chapter 23 'The Lord is my shepherd, I shall not want' is known as the chapter of livelihood and abundance.

questions.question: How many chapters are there in the book of Psalms?
questions.explanation: The book of Psalms contains 150 chapters, a number that symbolizes spiritual perfection.

closing.summary: We learned today the fundamentals of Psalms — how to choose a chapter according to age, month or specific need. We saw how Psalms connect to astrology and Kabbalah, and how they work as a scientific spiritual system.
closing.wisdom: King David didn't write beautiful poems — he created spiritual keys for every situation in life.
closing.nextPreview: In the next lesson we'll enter the first chapters — chapters 1 to 10, and learn the specific segulot (spiritual remedies) of each one.

faq.question: Is it mandatory to say the chapter according to age every day?
faq.answer: It's not mandatory, but it's a beautiful tradition that strengthens the connection between your soul and your current life stage.

faq.question: What do I do if I don't know which Hebrew month we're in?
faq.answer: You can always check a Hebrew calendar or Jewish apps. The Hebrew month changes according to the moon, not the Gregorian calendar.

faq.question: Can I say several chapters together?
faq.answer: Absolutely! Sometimes combining chapters creates a stronger spiritual effect. Start with one chapter and feel the influence.

faq.question: What's the difference between saying Psalms in Hebrew versus in translation?
faq.answer: In Hebrew there's stronger spiritual power because it's the original language, but even in translation there's an effect if you understand and direct your heart.

crossReferences.label: 🔤 The Power of Hebrew Letters in Kabbalah

seo.title: Psalms Lesson 1: Complete Guide to Choosing Chapters by Age and Month
seo.description: Learn how to choose which chapter of Psalms to recite according to age, Hebrew month and spiritual need. Baal Shem Tov tradition, segulot and connections to astrology and Kabbalah.
